note: SA scaling test on lumae 632 chunks. Compares 2-SA, 3-SA, 4-SA configs. SA3+SA4 share billing account.

code.note: code path also took hits at higher concurrency

interpretation.conclusion: Adding more SAs reduces wall time but does NOT linearly add Vertex headroom. At higher hyde_shard_count more requests fire simultaneously than the available quota can absorb, retry budgets exhaust, and chunks fail to ever complete. Net effect: faster but lossier. The 'fastest' run isn't the best.
interpretation.production_recommendation: Stick with NUM_SAS=2, hyde_shard_count=16 (Run A) for production. 97.9% hyde completion rate is acceptable; the gap can be filled by /hyde-enrich resumable retry. Higher shard counts trade data integrity for wall time — wrong tradeoff.
interpretation.billing_vs_project_question: Inconclusive from this test. To distinguish project-level vs billing-account quota, would need to (a) add longer Vertex retry windows so all configs complete eventually, then compare PURE wall time apples-to-apples, OR (b) test SA3 alone vs SA4 alone with same shard count and see if they hit the same RPM ceiling.
